title: This Week in Beaufort County
subject: Port Royal Sounder No. 2: this week’s events and the best specials we can verify
preheader: A full March 16–22 roundup, plus the weekly specials signals worth checking first.
intro: This issue is built from the live calendar plus the strongest weekly-specials signals we can verify right now from official sites. It includes everything currently on the calendar for March 16 through March 22, 2026, along with the food and seafood stops most worth checking this week.

items.name: Finance, Administration, and Economic Development Committee (T)
items.note: Monday, March 16 at 2:00 PM in Beaufort. County committee meeting for the week’s civic calendar.

items.name: Public Facilities Committee (T)
items.note: Monday, March 16 at 4:00 PM in Beaufort. Another county public meeting on the week’s docket.

items.name: St. Patrick's Party
items.note: Monday, March 16 at 7:00 PM. The Savannah Ghost Pirates host Toledo with a themed St. Patrick’s promotion.

items.name: Bluffton Township Fire District Board
items.note: Tuesday, March 17 at 4:00 PM. A public Bluffton fire district board meeting.

items.name: Birding at Widgeon Point: Land Preservation Nature Program
items.note: Wednesday, March 18 at 8:30 AM. A free county birding walk at Widgeon Point.

items.name: Transportation Advisory Committee
items.note: Wednesday, March 18 at 1:00 PM. Transportation advisory meeting in Beaufort.

items.name: Beaufort County Transportation Committee (T)
items.note: Wednesday, March 18 at 4:00 PM. Another transportation-focused public meeting for the week.

items.name: Historic Preservation Review Board
items.note: Thursday, March 19 at 1:00 PM. Historic preservation meeting in Beaufort.

items.name: Solid Waste and Recycling (T)
items.note: Thursday, March 19 at 2:00 PM. County board meeting in Beaufort.

items.name: Third Thursday - Historic Port Royal Foundation
items.note: Thursday, March 19 from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM. A recurring Port Royal community evening hosted by the Historic Port Royal Foundation.

items.name: Electric Avenue
items.location: USCB Center for the Arts, Beaufort
items.note: Thursday, March 19. USCB Center for the Arts is listing Electric Avenue, an 80's Movie Soundtrack Night, on its upcoming events page.

items.name: Brian Sella
items.note: Thursday, March 19. Music Farm is listing Brian Sella on its official upcoming events page.

items.name: Galactic Bowling
items.note: Thursday, March 19 from 8:00 PM to close. Station 300 lists $2 games and $2 shoe rental on Thursdays.

items.name: 4th Annual Spring Symposium Opening Reception
items.location: USCB Center for the Arts, Beaufort
items.note: Friday, March 20 at 6:00 PM. Opening reception for USCB’s annual spring symposium.

items.name: Greenville Swamp Rabbits @ Savannah Ghost Pirates
items.note: Friday, March 20 at 7:00 PM. Ghost Pirates home game in Savannah.

items.name: Galactic Bowling
items.note: Friday, March 20 from 8:00 PM to close. Friday nights are listed at $20.99 per person for two hours, with shoes included.

items.name: Weird Science on the inside stage
items.note: Friday, March 20 at 9:00 PM. The Windjammer is listing Weird Science on the inside stage on its official upcoming events page.

items.name: 4th Annual Spring Symposium
items.location: USCB Center for the Arts, Beaufort
items.note: Saturday, March 21 from 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM. Full day of symposium programming in Beaufort.

items.name: Spring Architects’ Tour
items.note: Saturday, March 21 from 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Historic Beaufort Foundation’s annual tour of distinctive homes and architecture.

items.name: Ghost Parrots Night
items.note: Saturday, March 21 at 7:00 PM. Another Ghost Pirates home game, this time with the Ghost Parrots theme night.

items.name: Rock the 90's on the NÜTRL Beach Stage
items.note: Saturday, March 21 at 6:00 PM. The Windjammer is listing Rock the 90's on the NÜTRL Beach Stage on its official upcoming events page.

items.name: Girl Pop Night
items.note: Saturday, March 21. Music Farm is listing Girl Pop Night on its official upcoming events page.

items.name: Galactic Bowling
items.note: Saturday, March 21 from 8:00 PM to close. Saturday nights are also listed at $20.99 per person for two hours, with shoes included.

items.name: What's Up Chuck on the inside stage
items.note: Saturday, March 21 at 9:30 PM. The Windjammer is listing What's Up Chuck on the inside stage on its official upcoming events page.

items.name: Healthcare Heroes Appreciation
items.note: Sunday, March 22 at 3:00 PM. Ghost Pirates home game with a healthcare-appreciation promotion.

items.name: Gay Fish Company
items.note: The market page currently calls out wild-caught local shrimp, crab, Atlantic fish, oysters, mussels, and rotating market specials based on availability and seasonality.

items.name: Sea Eagle Market
items.note: Sea Eagle is currently listing 10 pounds of wild-caught East Coast white shrimp for $180, described as caught weekly while in season on its own boats.

items.name: Lady's Island Oyster
items.note: The order page currently lists farm-raised oysters at $1.75 each for pre-ordered farm pickup, or $3 each shucked at the Port Royal Farmers Market on Saturdays, with two days' notice requested.

items.name: Bluffton Oyster Company
items.note: The official site says the retail market is in full operation Monday through Saturday and highlights fresh oysters, clams, mussels, shrimp, scallops, fish, blue crabs, low-country boils to go, and advance holiday reservations.

items.name: Station 300 Bluffton
items.note: Not food, but a real weekly special: Galactic Bowling is posted as $2 games plus $2 shoes on Thursdays, and $20.99 per person for two hours on Friday and Saturday nights with shoes included.

title: Lowcountry Weekend Starter
subject: Port Royal Sounder No. 1: markets, oysters, and a few local favorites
preheader: A first pass at the kind of useful local roundup this newsletter can become.
intro: This first issue is a compiled draft built from the directory and calendar sources already in Port Royal Sounder. The idea is simple: a small handful of places to try, events worth catching, and a short list of sources we think are worth watching each week.

items.name: The Kitchen
items.note: A strong prepared-food stop for grab-and-go meals, coffee, baked goods, catering, and pantry extras.

items.name: Herban Market & Cafe
items.note: A scratch-made market cafe with breads, pastries, fresh food, and a natural-products feel that fits the local roundup well.

items.name: Cypress Wetlands
items.note: One of the easiest local walks for birding, especially when you want something beautiful and low-effort close to town.

items.name: Port Royal Farmers Market
items.note: The anchor weekly market in this project, with local produce, seafood, meat, cheese, honey, flowers, and bread.

items.name: Downtown Beaufort Farmers' Market
items.note: A useful downtown stop for produce and prepared foods if you want something central and easy to combine with errands.

items.name: Farmers' Market of Bluffton
items.note: Worth the drive when you want a larger Thursday mix of local farms, makers, and specialty vendors.

items.name: 2026 FFF Oyster Roast
items.note: Friday, March 13 at Live Oaks Park. A classic Port Royal-style community event and an easy first pick for the calendar.

items.name: Radiance Run 5K
items.note: Saturday, March 14. A local community race that fits the kind of civic and neighborhood event we want to surface.

items.name: Third Thursday - Historic Port Royal Foundation
items.note: Thursday, March 19. Exactly the kind of recurring local event that deserves a reliable home in both the calendar and newsletter.

items.name: 4th Annual Spring Symposium
items.note: March 20 and 21 at USCB Center for the Arts, with a more academic and cultural angle than the usual weekend roundup.

items.name: Town of Port Royal Calendar
items.note: One of the most useful official feeds for town events, meetings, and community announcements.

items.name: Visit Beaufort Events
items.note: Useful for larger public events and tourism-facing happenings that still matter for locals.

items.name: USCB Center for the Arts
items.note: A strong arts and performance source that should likely feed both the newsletter and calendar regularly.
